metadata.title: Turn a Vague Claude Prompt into a Six-Component Specification
metadata.note: CLI video: 10-beat spine B00–B09. B04 is slate. No audio.

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.

lines: Two Minutes to One Right Artifact

beats.narration_text: Every vague Claude prompt has six missing components. A two-minute specification rewrite turns three wrong drafts into one right artifact. The six components are not style choices — they are the evaluation criteria that make supervision possible.

shot.notes: hook card: 'Six missing components. Two minutes. One right artifact.' — CRIMSON on 'vague', teal on 'specification'

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Every vague Claude prompt has six missing components. A two-minute specification rewrite turns three wrong drafts into one right artifact. The six components ar

beats.narration_text: In the terminal: ask Claude to build prompt_spec.py — a CLI that takes a weak prompt and a domain description, then outputs all six components with a before-and-after word-count table.

props.command:
claude "Write prompt_spec.py:
  takes weak_prompt and domain as args
  outputs six components:
    1. task (one sentence)
    2. context (who/what/why)
    3. source material
    4. constraints (what NOT to do)
    5. output format (exact structure)
    6. evaluation criteria (measurable)
  Include before/after word-count table."

beats.narration_text: Check the code. Constraints are negative — what Claude must not do — and distinct from the task. Evaluation criteria must be measurable: 'good' is not a criterion. These two components are the ones most writers skip, and their absence is what makes supervision impossible.

props.code:
# prompt_spec.py — Claude Code output
import argparse, textwrap

def specify(prompt, domain):
    return {
      'task':       f'[one sentence from: {prompt[:40]}]',
      'context':    f'[who/what/why in {domain}]',
      'source':     '[data/docs Claude should use]',
      'constraints':'[what it must NOT do]',
      'format':     '[exact output structure]',
      'criteria':   '[measurable pass/fail check]'
    }

if __name__ == '__main__':
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    p.add_argument('prompt'); p.add_argument('domain')
    args = p.parse_args()
    spec = specify(args.prompt, args.domain)
    for k, v in spec.items(): print(f'{k:12}: {v}')

beats.narration_text: Run it on the book's grant-report example. Weak prompt: 'write a grant report'. Six-component output: task, context, source material, constraints, output format, evaluation criteria — each on its own line, before-and-after word counts below.

shot.notes: terminal output showing six-component spec for grant-report example, before/after word count table — criteria column highlighted in teal

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Run it on the book's grant-report example. Weak prompt: 'write a grant report'. Six-component output: task, context, source material, constraints, output format

beats.narration_text: Add diagnosis mode. Give it an existing Claude output and a specification, and it identifies which of the six components was violated — rendered as a checklist.

props.command:
claude "update prompt_spec.py:
  add --diagnose mode
  takes existing Claude output + spec file
  identifies which of 6 components was violated
  render as pass/fail checklist"

beats.narration_text: Run the diagnosis on a real failure: a literature review that hallucinated citations. The checklist prints: task — pass, context — pass, source material — fail, constraints — pass, output format — pass, evaluation criteria — fail. Two components. That is where the supervision gap was.

shot.notes: terminal: diagnosis checklist — task PASS, context PASS, source material FAIL (CRIMSON), constraints PASS, format PASS, criteria FAIL (CRIMSON) — two failures surfaced

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Run the diagnosis on a real failure: a literature review that hallucinated citations. The checklist prints: task — pass, context — pass, source material — fail,

beats.narration_text: Prompting as specification is not about longer prompts. It is about making the evaluation criteria legible before the artifact is produced. The criteria you cannot write are the ones you cannot supervise.

shot.notes: teardown card: 'Longer prompt ≠ better spec' (CRIMSON) vs 'Legible evaluation criteria = supervisable output' (teal) — the teardown distinction

beats.narration_text: Your move: run your last three Claude prompts through prompt_spec.py and check which component is missing. Next reel: the engineering partner loop — where Claude fixes code and the oracle verifies the fix.
